definition: Distraction through aversion, hostility, or ill will toward what is resisted.
notes: Project preference is aversive distraction within the nīvaraṇa framework so the relation to distraction remains explicit. Ill will remains a clear alternate when explanatory prose wants the more familiar ethical label.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing hostility toward others or situations
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the aversive tone needs a more familiar phrase.

example_phrases.notes: Standard member of the fivefold list.

example_phrases.translation: a mind free from aversive distraction
